subject: resnet8-cifar10-int4-weight-only
notes: Full CIFAR-10 test set (10000 images, 1000/class), matches docs/record_format.md's 10,000-record count. Images from the uoft-cs/cifar10 HF Parquet mirror (lossless PNG bytes) -- see module docstring for why a JPEG mirror was rejected (measured ~13-point accuracy hit from lossy re-compression on this 32x32-input model). MLPerf Tiny reference reports ~87.0% top-1 on its own 200-sample perf subset; quality target 85%+ top-1 on the full test set. Weight-only NNCF compression (nncf.compress_weights) -- activations and the matmul itself still execute fp32/fp16 on OpenVINO CPU, so this is a real accuracy number but NOT a measured or achievable throughput point; scripts/make_pareto.py projects its x-axis compute multiplier from issue #10's L0b soft-logic MAC densities, not from this run.
